ocr.text: In Table 1, the region "Inferior temporal gyrus" has a cluster of 26, located on the left (L), with a value of 8.25 and MNI
coordinates of (−42, −13, −35). The next entry is a continuation without a specific region label, having a cluster of —, L/R as
—, a value of 6.02, and MNI coordinates of (−48, −16, −29). The "Inferior frontal triangularis" region has a cluster of 23,
located on the right (R), with a value of 7.75 and MNI coordinates of (57, 35, 7).

The "Caudate/Insula" region has a cluster of 22, located on the left (L), with a value of 7.69 and MNI coordinates of (−21, 20,
22). This is followed by another entry without a specific region label, having a cluster of —, L/R as —, a value of 7.34, and
MNI coordinates of (−15, 26, 25). The final entry features a missing region label, with a cluster of —, L/R as —, a value of
7.27, and MNI coordinates of (−21, 26, 10). Table 2 lists evaluated biomarkers where "CSF" includes MMP-2 and MMP-9 as
markers of BBB dysfunction, followed by neuroaxonal degeneration markers NfL, pNFH/C3, p-tau, neuroprotection markers
cystatin C and TDP-43, inflammation and immune activation markers including IL-6, IL-8, complement factors C3 and C4,
prostaglandin E2, neopterin, peroxynitrite, G-CSF, MCP-1, glial activation markers S100b and EPO, followed by blood markers
creatinine, albumin, ferritin, and muscle markers Nogo-A and smads.
